The item added to the stack last will be the item removed last.

questions.question: For each of the following situations, select the appropriate or best data structure and justify your selection.
questions.modelAnswer:
i. Queue: customers who come first are served first, so FIFO fits.
ii. Double Linked List: browser navigation needs previous and next movement.
iii. Stack: undo-redo reverses operations, so LIFO fits.
iv. Binary Search Tree: sorted items can be searched efficiently.

subQuestions.label: i
subQuestions.prompt: Your structure will be used in a shop system where customers who come first will order first.

subQuestions.label: ii
subQuestions.prompt: Your structure will be used in a web browser where users need to access previous and next URLs by pressing back and next.

subQuestions.label: iii
subQuestions.prompt: Your structure will be used by an application that remembers operations in reverse order to perform undo and redo functionality.

subQuestions.label: iv
subQuestions.prompt: Your structure will be used by an address book where items are already sorted and optimal search is required.

questions.question:
Given the figure below, answer the following questions.

50
/ \
17 76
/ \ /
9 23 54
\ \ \
14 19 72
/ /
12 67
questions.modelAnswer:
i. No, it is not balanced because the subtree rooted at 9 is not height-balanced.
ii. No, it is not a Binary Search Tree because node 19 appears in the right subtree of 23 while 19 < 23.
iii. Post-order traversal: 12, 14, 9, 19, 23, 17, 67, 72, 54, 76, 50.
iv. There is no unique BST-style replacement root here because the given tree is not a valid BST. If BST deletion rules were applied hypothetically, the inorder successor of 50 would be 54.

subQuestions.label: ii
subQuestions.prompt: Is this a Binary Search Tree?

subQuestions.label: iii
subQuestions.prompt: Write the post-order traversal of this tree.

subQuestions.label: iv
subQuestions.prompt: If the root 50 is deleted, what will be the new root?

questions.question: Write a C++ template class for a double-ended queue (Deque) implemented as a doubly linked list.

subQuestions.label: a
subQuestions.prompt: Include the Node class with its constructor and the Deque class with its constructor.

subQuestions.label: b
subQuestions.prompt: Implement pushFront(val), which adds a value val to the front of the queue.

subQuestions.label: c
subQuestions.prompt: Implement popBack(), which removes the value at the back of the queue.

subQuestions.label: d
subQuestions.prompt: Implement moveToFront(pos), which moves the value at position pos to the front of the queue.

questions.question: An Amazon-like company continuously receives a stream of transactions. Each transaction means that a person bought one unit of a product identified by its unique ID. At any point, the system should print the sales count of a product and the top five sold products.
